free_text: Photoneutron angular distributions for He-4.
Electron  beam characteristics:  peak
current = 0.4 amps,  pulse width=15 nsec, repetition
rate = 360 hz.
The bremsstrahlung target consisted of 0.20 cm
thick  tungsten  plate  backed  by  a  4.45 cm thick
aluminium electron beam stop.
The liquid  helium sample dewar.  The irradiated part
of the sample dewar was 5.08 cm in diameter and  was
constructed of 0.25 mm aluminium walls.
Flight path=49.5 m.
The  neutron  detector  consists  of an oxygen
poisoned liquid scintillator (12.7 cm thick by  35.6
c  in  diameter,  ne  213)  viewed  by three philips
xp1040 photomultiplier tubes.
The bremsstrahlung    shape    is    obtained    from
measurements of photoneutron spectra  from  0.64  cm
thick heavy water (D2O) and water (H2O) slab targets
contained in holders with 0.01 cm thick mylar walls.
The statistical uncertainties.
The data were  corrected for
(a) the neutrons produced in the aluminium;
(b) the neutrons scattered by the helium sample;
(c) the sample thickness.
The double  differential  cross   section   for   the
reaction   He-4(g,n)He-3   has   been   measured  at
laboratory angle of 53,68,83,98,128 and 143  degrees
for excitation energies between 22 and 32 MeV by the
photoneutron time-of-flight technique.
The photoneutron spectra  were  used  to  obtain  the
angular  distribution  coefficients  in  a  legendre
series expansion for the  differential  photoneutron
cross   sections.  Analysis  of  these  coefficients
indicates that the  photodisintegration  process  in
this   nucleus,   while   dominated   by  E1  photon
absorption followed by channel  spin  zero (no  spin
flip)  p-wave  neutron  emission,  is affected by E1
channel spin one and E2 contributions. In the higher
energy region E > 27 MeV the amount of E2 absorption
observed was 6% of the total and is dominated by the
channel spin one E2 matrix element.
The       fore-aft
asymmetry is supported by the present data.
Data were compiled at  SCJAD  of  Saratov
State University.
Corrected by V.Varlamov, V.Mclane.

Data from Fig. 4.
To obtain the cross sections the photoneutron
spectra had been aligned to common  center  of  mass
energy    bins    and   normalized   to   the   same
bremsstrahlung  dose.  The   cross   sections   were
obtained  by dividing the spectra by the appropriate
bremsstrahlung  distributions.   The   total   cross
section  was  obtained  by  normalizing to the cross
section measured by Irish, et al.
Data from Fig. 5.
